title: Ensure that the admission control policy is set to NamespaceLifecycle

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control policy is set to NamespaceLifecycle
input_row.description: Reject creating objects in a namespace that is undergoing termination.
input_row.rationale: Setting admission control policy to NamespaceLifecycle ensures that the namespaces undergoing termination are not used for creating the new objects. This is recommended to enforce the integrity of the namespace termination process and also for the availability of the newer objects.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-apiserver Verify that the --admission-control argument is set to a value that includes NamespaceLifecycle.
input_row.remediation: Edit the deployment specs and set --admission-control argument to a value that includes NamespaceLifecycle. kubectl edit deployments federation-apiserver-deployment -- namespace=federation-system Impact: None Default Value: By default, NamespaceLifecycle is set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/federation-apiserver/  201 | P a g e 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/federation/manifests/fe deration-apiserver-deployment.yaml 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/
input_row.impact: None Default Value: By default, NamespaceLifecycle is set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugin_namespacelifecycle_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the Kubernetes cluster for the deployment or pod specification of the federation-apiserver (or kube-apiserver, if applicable).
- Use the Kubernetes API or kubectl to retrieve the command-line arguments of the federation-apiserver container.
  - Example command:  
    ```
    kubectl -n federation-system get deployment federation-apiserver-deployment -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[*].command}'
    ```
    or, if using pods directly:
    ```
    kubectl -n federation-system get pods -l app=federation-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
    ```
- Parse the command line for the presence of the `--admission-control` or `--enable-admission-plugins` flag.
- Confirm that the value for this flag includes `NamespaceLifecycle` in the comma-separated list of admission plugins.
- Pass Criteria: The `NamespaceLifecycle` plugin is present in the admission control plugins list.
- Fail Criteria: The `NamespaceLifecycle` plugin is missing from the admission control plugins list.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
